narrative: Pearl extended its run: volume-weighted PRL sits at $0.9172 (+37.9% 24h) on ~$76.4k of cross-venue volume, lifting fully-circulating cap to ~$154M. Network hashrate climbed 15.94 → 17.93 EH/s and ~120s block cadence held, even though MinePRL pool workers fell 79 → 56 — net hash kept rising, so capacity migrated to other miners rather than disappearing. Bridge surplus drew down hard: ~10.6k PRL left the reserve while minted WPRL stayed essentially flat (148.5k → 149.3k), pulling collateralization back from 8.26% to 1.08%. Public-frontend (28) and PearlWallet (7) shipped fewer commits week-over-week, but the L1 repo (pearl-research-labs/pearl) ticked from 83 → 90 stars as the price move drew eyes.

market.prl_circulating_supply_method: block_height × current_block_reward (constant-subsidy approximation — Pearl is in month 1, before the first halving)
sources: dexscreener:tokens/0x07696DcaB55E62cfef953666b29Fe1970518cB00 (3 Ethereum pairs aggregated, volume-weighted price/change — the Uniswap v4 WPRL/USDT pool that appeared yesterday was not returned in today’s search, so we exclude it rather than carry stale numbers)

network.active_workers_scope: MinePRL pool only — network-wide miner count is not exposed by Pearl RPC

anomalies: Bridge surplus drew down sharply (8.26% → 1.08%): custody fell ~9.86k PRL while minted WPRL only changed +0.79k — ~10.6k PRL net left the surplus reserve in 24h. Collateralization stays >100% but reserve buffer is now thin; worth a confirmation from bridge ops that this was a planned drawdown.
Pool worker count fell (79 → 56) while network hashrate rose (+12.5%) — capacity migrated to other miners; not a network-health concern, just a scope-of-MinePRL note.

social.summary: X API not exercised this run — but the +37.9% 24h move and steady $PRL price-chart posts on @prlnet are consistent with bullish chatter. Carrying sentiment forward conservatively until a sampled-tweets run lands.

github.last_commit_msg: bug fix for bitcoin utils package serializing OP_PUSHDATA1 opcode (#115)
merge next → main: ecosystem schema v2 + mobile T&C
